title: cyperus sanguinolentus f. melanocephalus
intro: Cyperus sanguinolentus f. melanocephalus, also known as the Black-headed Papyrus Sedge, is a subspecies of the Cyperus sanguinolentus plant, which is a type of sedge that belongs to the Cyperaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe and treat various health issues. The Black-headed Papyrus Sedge has been traditionally used to treat a range of health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
